coaching.title: Hill Repeats — 8 mi (Recovery Week)
coaching.message: Today's session: 2 mi easy warm-up, 10x 60-sec uphills, 2 mi cool-down — 8 mi total. It's a recovery week, so this is controlled, NOT all-out: aim Zone 3 on the climbs (~150-160 bpm, well under your 165 LTHR) and let HR fully settle on the walk-down. Drive with short, powerful steps and a tall chest — that's the exact power-hiking-to-running transition you'll need on the Julian Alps' ~2500m of vert. Form over effort this week; you're absorbing the last three weeks of build, not adding to it.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Short, intense-ish session, so keep it simple: 30-40g easy carbs 60-90 min before (banana + toast or a gel). Under an hour you don't need much on the move — water with electrolytes is plenty. Within 30 min of finishing, hit ~25-30g protein plus carbs to lock in the adaptation. On this lighter day, anchor around ~220g carbs total and lean into anti-inflammatory foods (berries, fatty fish, leafy greens) to support the recovery-week goal.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: The climbs load your calves, glutes and Achilles hard. Post-run: 10 min easy walk to flush, then foam-roll calves and quads and 5 min of ankle/calf mobility. This is a recovery week — protect sleep (aim 8+ hrs) and resist the urge to add 'bonus' volume. You're traveling, so prioritize legs-up and hydration over anything heroic.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: 92 days out, mid-build, recovery week — this is where disciplined athletes separate themselves by doing LESS on purpose. Every controlled climb today is a rep at the patience the Julian Alps will demand when the trail tilts up and the smart move is to hike, not hammer. Trust the plan: holding back this week is what lets you peak in August.
